author: role.analyst.classic-3d-systems

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player grows from an isolated child into a time-crossing hero whose competence is expressed by reading a three-dimensional world and helping its communities.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: Follow a lead from the central field or a settlement, enter a bounded challenge, learn an item rule, defeat an exam encounter, and use the new capability to reinterpret earlier routes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Analog movement, context actions, lock-on sword and shield combat, aimed tools, object handling, riding, and performed melodies share a compact action vocabulary.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Child and adult eras expose different occupants, hazards, routes, and affordances while selected quest progress and acquired capabilities persist across the transition.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: A broad field works as a memorable hub whose visible exits lead to settlements and contained dungeons, with landmarks and route repetition building spatial memory.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Items, songs, age, story permissions, and learned environmental rules gate progress; shortcuts and changed-era routes make return travel materially different.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Puzzles present manipulable objects, targetable features, audio or visual cues, and item-compatible surfaces so a hypothesis can be attempted and immediately confirmed.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Room-and-key dependency graphs introduce a local rule, add navigation pressure around a central device or spatial theme, and end with an item-centered boss exam.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Optional routes yield health pieces, bottles, collectible tokens, songs, ammunition capacity, and small discoveries that expand either endurance or future possibility.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Health, magic, wallets, ammunition limits, bottles, and durable tools grow over the adventure while rupees and consumables support moment-to-moment preparation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Lock-on stabilizes facing and camera, enabling circling, guarding, timed attacks, projectile aiming, and bosses whose tells expose a tool-mediated vulnerability.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Characters supply local goals, trade steps, songs, and state-dependent responses; day, place, and age sometimes change who is available and what can be completed.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: A youthful collection quest pivots into an adult restoration journey, with era changes, location transformations, and short staged reveals carrying much of the drama.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: Third-person framing, lock-on recentering, first-person aiming, entrance reveals, and selective fixed cameras keep actors, targets, and room-scale mechanisms readable.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: The opening settlement isolates movement, targeting, dialogue, currency, and equipment lessons; companions, action prompts, effects, and enemy reactions continue contextual guidance.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The N64 analog stick, Z trigger, context-sensitive action button, and three quick item buttons make mode and target state visible while limiting simultaneous tool loadout.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Saving, bottled fairies, health drops, dungeon shortcuts, and persistent major acquisitions soften failure, though the original version offers limited explicit assistance.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: Full 3D creates depth aiming, camera occlusion, vertical landmarks, and free approach angles, requiring targeting and contextual movement to reduce orientation burden.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The original is single-player; its design is materially shaped by one analog stick, a rear targeting trigger, and the N64 controller's separate item buttons.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: A faithful abstraction needs target-aware locomotion and camera, room state graphs, era variants, persistent inventory and quest flags, melody recognition, and robust transition staging.

observationThemes.context: A hostile actor is within targeting range in a navigable 3D encounter space.
preconditions: A valid target is visible or near the camera's focus.
playerInput: Hold the targeting trigger and choose attack, guard, dodge, or aimed item.
before: Facing and camera direction can diverge from the opponent.
Depth makes lateral relation harder to judge.
action: Acquire the target and execute a combat verb relative to it.
after: Facing and framing remain anchored to the opponent.
Movement becomes orbiting and distance management.
feedback: Target marker, camera recentering, character facing, guard reactions, and hit effects expose the current relation.
consequences: The player can reason about timing and spacing instead of continuously correcting the camera.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The original game binds targeting to the rear trigger and uses it for enemy combat, distant character interaction, and target-relative movement.
observationThemes.interpretation: A temporary relational mode can make a new spatial dimension manageable without removing free movement outside the encounter.

observationThemes.context: The player has learned the hub and several settlements in one age and later gains access to another age.
preconditions: The corresponding location has been visited before.
The age transition is available at its controlled access point.
playerInput: Cross eras and return to a known route or settlement.
before: The player owns a spatial model of the younger-era route.
Some paths or actors are unavailable.
action: Re-enter the same named place in the other era and test its changed affordances.
after: Landmarks remain recognizable while hazards, residents, access, and route uses differ.
feedback: Changed lighting, occupancy, geometry state, music, dialogue, and blocked or opened paths make the era legible.
consequences: Old navigation knowledge reduces orientation cost while changed state creates new questions and gates.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The game lets the hero occupy child and adult eras, and many shared locations differ in population, danger, and accessible actions between them.
observationThemes.interpretation: Keeping landmark identity stable while changing affordances turns revisit into comparison rather than repetition.

observationThemes.context: Inside a bounded dungeon, a newly acquired tool matches previously seen but unresolved features.
preconditions: The player has encountered a blocked dependency.
The dungeon item has just been acquired.
playerInput: Apply the item to a nearby safe example, then to earlier or more complex obstacles.
before: The player's tool vocabulary lacks the matching verb.
action: Use the tool on signaled targets and combine it with navigation or combat.
after: A route, reward, or mechanism changes state.
The item remains available beyond the dungeon.
feedback: Distinct target reaction, sound, animation, opened geometry, and map progress confirm the rule.
consequences: The same acquisition resolves the local dependency graph and expands later overworld possibility.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Major dungeons place a durable item inside their route and repeatedly require it before using related interactions in a culminating encounter.
observationThemes.interpretation: An acquisition feels consequential when it is taught locally, examined structurally, and then exported into the wider world.

observationThemes.context: A character or marked place teaches or calls for a short melody tied to a world function.
preconditions: The melody has been learned or can be imitated from the scene.
playerInput: Enter instrument mode and perform the note sequence with the controller buttons.
before: A social promise, route, time state, or travel need is unresolved.
after: A scripted response, state change, or transport operation begins.
feedback: The view isolates the performance, notes sound as entered, and recognition triggers a distinctive scene transition.
consequences: Remembered input becomes both a player-owned command and a recurring narrative motif.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Learned melodies are entered through controller buttons and can affect travel, time, weather-like conditions, characters, or story events depending on context.
observationThemes.interpretation: Requiring a tiny performance before a world command can bind interface memory to dramatic meaning.

observationThemes.context: A dungeon ends after the player has practiced its item and encounter grammar in lower-risk rooms.
preconditions: The player has seen compatible targets or tells.
playerInput: Read the boss tell, use the matching item or positioning response, then exploit the exposed opening.
before: The boss is protected or cannot be damaged efficiently.
action: Trigger the taught vulnerability cycle and attack during its confirmed window.
after: The boss advances damage or phase state while the player retains a recoverable combat loop.
feedback: Camera emphasis, sound, animation, target state, and damage reaction distinguish failed reads from valid openings.
consequences: Success validates the dungeon's grammar and awards durable progression; failure can be retried without replaying the whole dungeon.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Dungeon bosses use staged tells and vulnerabilities, commonly involving the local item, and the dungeon retains major route progress after combat failure.
observationThemes.interpretation: A boss can function as a comprehension exam when its state machine makes the taught rule legible and preserves the costliest prior learning.

contributionHypotheses: A reversible world-age layer can make stable landmarks support substantial revisit without demanding an entirely separate map.
A target-relative interaction mode may unify combat, conversation, and camera control in free 3D spaces.
A short performed input phrase can operate as both diegetic ritual and reusable system command.
uniqueOrNonportable: The exact child-to-adult chronology, sacred instrument, melody set, named peoples, temples, and plot revelations are recognizable authored surface content and must not be reused.
Specific dungeon layouts, boss sequences, iconography, audiovisual cues, and the Hyrule Field arrangement are nonportable.
Z-targeting's branded presentation and N64 button mapping are historical implementation particulars; only the abstract target-relative relation may be compared.
openQuestions: The announced 2026 Nintendo Switch 2 edition remains outside this original-version behavior profile until Nintendo publishes its edition type and changes.
A later review should separate original regional save behavior and text differences if recovery is compared at that granularity.
